mentions.note: Cheap at 16x earnings, but Cramer wants to wait for CEO Jamie Dimon's characteristically cautious earnings-call commentary to knock the stock down before buying.
Worried about Jamie Dimon's cautious conference-call tone hitting the stock even if the quarter is strong; wait for a dip on the call, then buy.
Weaker-than-expected quarter and Dimon's caution on credit card fees triggered a selloff; Cramer expects a bounce back and says buy the weakness.
Solid top- and bottom-line beat; sell-off driven by Dimon's cautious commentary and a 35% prior run, not the underlying business.
Financials rallying on Trump's 'war won' comments and potential peace resolution, but Cramer uncertain given continued missile strikes.
Barely up despite expected rate-cut driven strength; financial sector disappointing.
Terrific bank but many important inputs to quarter; CEO loves caution so Wall Street finds quibbles even if unwarranted; earnings day Tuesday with Wells and Citi.
Blue-chip bank +9.7% earnings growth at 13.9× earnings; investment banking and asset gathering fueling growth; undervalued vs. S&P 500 despite lowest growth rate among majors.
Fortress balance sheet, 13x earnings (below market average), best-in-class franchise. Contrarian value entry amid credit fears; buy and hold, not a momentum play.
Real bank rallied strongly alongside PNC and Citi; strong bank performance signals healthy economy and positive consumer financial position.
Stock fundamentally fine but timing is wrong; wait until SpaceX IPO deal is fully priced before entering position.
Bank rally has staying power on moderately higher rates (NIM expansion), strong consumer (May retail +6.9% YoY), tame delinquencies; still cheap; 50%+ upside before reasonably priced.
Highest net income, revenue growth, largest deposit base, highest ROE, largest capital return, highest investment revenues of major banks; trades 15x earnings—best bank in world.
